title: WF-006 Courier Dispatch to Receipt — make the chain actually work end-to-end (+ fix server-generated codes)
business_reason: First workflow taken under the workflow-at-a-time rule (WF-006). The courier scaffold LOOKED built (pages, routes, nav, generated lifecycle transitions) but the chain did not actually work: creating a courier shipment failed with 'code Required'. Root cause: the courier tables use a bare `code` column (not `*_code`), so gen_modules (a) left it as a REQUIRED client field in createSchema and (b) never set codeColumn, so the kernel's server-side code generation never fired. Codes must be server-owned (P3.9 / mass-assignment). Fix the generator so any `code`/`*_code` column is server-generated, then prove the dispatch->receipt journey.

design.generator_fix: _validation/gen_modules.py: create_schema_fields now also EXCLUDES a bare `code` column (was only `*_code`), so it's never a client input; detect_code_column(table) finds the server-generated business code (`code` or `*_code`, NOT NULL, not already SERVER_COMPUTED) and gen_service emits codeColumn + a derived codePrefix, so the kernel auto-generates PREFIX-<uuid8> on create. Generalizes to 53 services (fixes a latent create-time bug wherever a generated module is created via API).
design.chain: courier_ops_shipments lifecycle: create (server code) -> mark_in_transit -> deliver -> receive; courier_ops_exceptions: raise -> start_review -> resolve. Pages + routes + nav already generated (P0.10 satisfied).
design.ui: Already wired: /staff/courier (+ Shipments / Receipts / Exceptions / Dispatch Batches) generated ModuleTable pages with transition actions, nav-linked.

param_14.question: How does a dispatched courier shipment reach 'received', and how are incidents handled?
param_14.answer: Staff dispatch a shipment (its code is server-generated, never client-supplied), then track it mark_in_transit -> delivered -> received. If there's a mismatch/loss, a courier exception (incident) is raised and worked open -> under_review -> resolved. All via the generated staff courier pages; each transition is audited.
param_14.answer_source: courier_ops workflows/lifecycle + courier_shipments/courier_exceptions schema; WORKFLOW_REGISTRY WF-006
param_14.security_implications: FIXED a mass-assignment smell — business codes are now server-generated for every module, never accepted from the client (P3.9). Staff-scoped + audited transitions.
param_14.approval_implications: receipt confirm/reject and exception resolution remain staff actions (klass approve where reason-required).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: tie receive -> reconcile sheets_received vs expected and auto-raise an exception on mismatch (effect); link a shipment to its exam_material_package; school-side receipt confirmation.
param_14.assumptions: the shipment lifecycle is the dispatch->receipt spine; courier_exceptions is the incident entity.

verify: npx playwright test courier_dispatch (+ full regression for the 53-service codeColumn change)
